newsSinceJun6: No new fundamental news. June 8: Rosen Law Firm reminder of the June 29 lead-plaintiff deadline in the securities class action (recycled litigation headline, covers the Apr 1-27 class period tied to the CFO confidentiality breach and PFIC misstatements). Today's -12.8% to $10.71 (~-33% over two sessions) is flow-driven: continuation of the Marvell/Celestial AI order-cancellation overhang, lawsuit headlines, and semis-complex weakness (Broadcom guidance, Marvell -17% Friday) - no contracts, earnings, or guidance. Live catalyst ahead: June 26 AGM vote on US redomiciliation to shed the PFIC stigma; balance sheet buffered by the recently closed $400M investment.

sources.label: Rosen Law June 8 lead-plaintiff deadline reminder (Morningstar/PR Newswire)
POET's June tightrope: June 26 redomicile vote vs June 29 class-action deadline (ad-hoc-news)
POET stock hit Friday; semis selloff and litigation context (ts2.tech)
Lawsuits mount after 47% collapse on Marvell/Celestial AI order cancellation (timothysykes.com)

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's de-rate to 17 already priced the Celestial/Marvell fallout; since then the stock flushed another ~33% over two sessions (-12.8% today) on recycled lawsuit reminders and semis beta, not new fundamental damage. On this page's asymmetry lens, a flow-driven crash with the optionality thesis intact improves residual alpha into the June 26 redomicile vote.
verdicts.bull: Optical interposer platform and CPO optionality are intact, $400M fresh cash funds the rebuild, and a successful June 26 US redomiciliation removes the PFIC stigma that has kept institutions out - all at a price ~48% below the May high.
verdicts.neutral: The disclosure-risk leg of the thesis already materialized; optionality survives but the order book must be rebuilt from near zero, and the multiple stays capped until the June 29 lead-plaintiff deadline and litigation scope clarify.
verdicts.bear: The Marvell cancellation gutted the only marquee customer pipeline, class actions and CFO credibility damage deter new design wins, and $503K quarterly revenue against a $12.3M quarterly loss gives little fundamental floor if the tape stays risk-off.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 26 AGM vote on US redomiciliation (PFIC overhang removal) plus any new customer or design-win announcement replacing the canceled Celestial AI orders
verdicts.primaryRisk: Securities-litigation escalation and failure to replace the Celestial AI order book before cash-burn pressure returns

verdicts.rankRationale: No new bottleneck-relevant evidence since June 6: the June 8 Rosen item is a recycled deadline reminder and today's -12.8% is flow/semis beta, which the scaling lens looks through. POET's optical-interposer role in AI networking is unchanged, though rank 3 increasingly leans rich for a sub-$1M-revenue name that lost its marquee customer in April.
verdicts.bull: The optical interposer remains a credible answer to the AI networking/packaging bottleneck, and $400M of cash plus a new COO (ex-Silicon Labs) give it runway to qualify replacement customers.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck fit is a technology claim, not yet a revenue claim - with Celestial AI orders canceled, POET's actual position in the AI supply chain is aspirational until a new anchor customer is announced.
verdicts.bear: A company with $503K quarterly revenue, a terminated lead-customer relationship, and management consumed by class actions and a redomicile vote is not a load-bearing node in the scaling supply chain; rank 3 overstates its centrality.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A replacement anchor-customer or hyperscaler qualification announcement validating the interposer in production AI networking
verdicts.primaryRisk: Prolonged customer-pipeline vacuum post-Celestial that exposes rank 3 as resting on technology promise rather than supply-chain position
